thermal_caveat: All numbers are first-run-cold-machine. M3 Max throttles to ~3x slower after a few seconds of sustained load; rerun on a cool machine for clean readings (see feedback_thermal_perf_artifacts.md).

prefill.path: predict_q4k_prefill (legacy per-layer dequant + dense f32 sgemv)

interpretation.decode_gap_is_kernel_work: Effective Q4_K weight bandwidth on M3 Max: larql ~30 GB/s, llama.cpp ~95 GB/s. Both are well below LPDDR5 peak (~400 GB/s). Closing the rest is hand-written NEON with multi-superblock interleaving, prefetch, and removing per-matvec rayon launches.
interpretation.prefill_gap_is_path_choice: larql's prefill goes through the legacy dequantise-then-sgemv path. ~75 ms/layer × 33 layers ≈ 2.5 s of dequant. llama.cpp uses batched q4k_matmul that reads each weight once and amortises across seq_len positions. Routing larql's prefill through a CPU q4k_matmul (which the backend trait already declares but doesn't implement) is the next big-bang win — comparable in size to the entire decode-optimisation arc on this branch.
